doc: Hetzner box (n8n-server) listening-port baseline for the monthly security sweep S5. The sweep captures `ss -tlnp` and diffs against this expected set; any NEW public listener not here is flagged. Captured over ssh; process names need root, docker-published ports resolved against `docker ps`. Localhost-only + docker-internal ports are NOT attack surface. Regenerate this baseline deliberately (not automatically) whenever the box's intended topology changes (new published container port, SSH move, etc).
captured_by: P5 security sweep S5 - first baseline

public_listeners.purpose: HTTPS - Caddy terminates TLS, reverse-proxies n8n + Alex HQ + MCP

localhost_only.purpose: n8n editor/API - NOT public, only reachable through Caddy on 443

docker_internal_not_published.note: n8n Postgres backend - internal to the docker network only, NOT host-published (absent from ss)
PDF render service for #03/#14 - docker-internal only
Alex HQ Next.js app - fronted by Caddy, docker-internal only

verdict: CLEAN 2026-07-18. Only 22/80/443 face the internet; every app port (n8n 5678, Postgres 5432, Gotenberg/HQ 3000) is bound to localhost or the docker network and reached only through Caddy. No unexpected public listener. This is the intended topology.
